'''Ronan Michael''' (born 3 July 2000) is an Irish professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Innisfail, Queensland|Innisfail Leprachauns]] in the [[Cairns District Rugby League]] and {{rlnt|Ireland}} at international level.

He previously played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]], [[York City Knights|York]] and the [[Swinton Lions]].

==Background==
Born in [[Balbriggan]] to a Canadian-Irish mother and an Antiguan father, Michael grew up playing [[rugby union]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/west-indies-rugby-league-team|title=How the West Indies could line-up if their rugby league team returned|date=10 January 2023|website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref name=revelling-IrishTimes>{{cite web|last=Watterson|first=Johnny|date=7 May 2021|title=Ronan Michael revelling in his sporting life after landing in Super League
|url=https://www.irishtimes.com/sport/rugby/ronan-michael-revelling-in-his-sporting-life-after-landing-in-super-league-1.4553560|work=Irish Times|access-date=27 June 2021}}</ref>

He began playing rugby league in 2017 for [[Ireland National League (rugby league)|Longhorns RL]] and was selected to play for {{rlnt|Ireland}} in 2018 and 2019.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ireland-international-ronan-michael-on-his-wish-to-see-a-professional-team-in-dublin/ |title=Ireland international Ronan Michael on his wish to see a professional team in Dublin |publisher=Love Rugby League |first=Drew |last=Darbyshire |date=5 October 2020}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Huddersfield Giants===
Michael signed an academy contract with the [[Huddersfield Giants]] for the 2019 season.<ref>{{cite web|date=25 October 2018|title=From the Leaving Cert to the Huddersfield Giants - Ronan Michael's story|url=https://www.otbsports.com/videos/from-the-leaving-cert-to-the-huddersfield-giants-ronan-michaels-story-236409|work=Off The Ball Sports|access-date=27 June 2021}}</ref>

Michael moved to Australia in January 2020 to play for the [[Canberra Raiders]] in their [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] team (under-20s). He remained contracted to Huddersfield for 2021.<ref>{{cite web|date=8 January 2020|title=Michael takes NRL step|url=https://www.giantsrl.com/article/7169/michael-takes-nrl-step|website=giantsrl.com|access-date=27 June 2021}}</ref> He did not play any competition matches for Canberra as the season was cancelled due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic in Australia|COVID-19 pandemic]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Walker|first=Gareth|date=17 March 2021|title=The remarkable rugby league story of Ireland international Ronan Michael|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/remarkable-rugby-league-story-ireland-23746067|work=Mirror|access-date=27 June 2021}}</ref> He returned home in March 2020.<ref name="washing windows to SL" />

Michael was named to make his [[Super League]] debut for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] against the [[Wigan Warriors]] in round 20 of the [[Super League XXV|2020 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ladbible.com/news/sport-ronan-michael-from-athboy-could-make-his-debut-tomorrow-20201105 |title=Ronan Michael set to be first native-Irish Super League player in over a decade |publisher=Lad Bible |first=Mike |last=Wood |date=5 November 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/irish-star-ronan-michael-set-to-make-super-league-debut-on-friday/ |title=Irish star Ronan Michael set to make Super League debut on Friday |publisher=Love Rugby League |date=5 November 2020}}</ref> He was the first Irish-born player to play in the Super League since [[Brian Carney (rugby)|Brian Carney]] in 2009.<ref name="washing windows to SL">{{cite web|last=Findlater|first=Stephen|date=24 November 2020|title=From washing windows to a Super League debut: How Ronan Michael became a Giant|url=https://www.irishexaminer.com/sport/rugby/arid-40088834.html|work=Irish Examiner|access-date=27 June 2021}}</ref>

===Whitehaven (loan)===
On 29 April 2021 he signed for [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Serious About RL|date=29 Apr 2021|accessdate=30 Apr 2021|title=Super League star joins Whitehaven on-loan
|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/super-league-star-joins-whitehaven-on-loan-39068/}}</ref>

===Swinton Lions (loan)===
On 27 May 2021 he signed for [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Swinton Lions RLFC|date=27 May 2021|accessdate=30 Aug 2021|title=GIANT RONAN JOINS THE LIONS|url=https://swintonlionsrlfc.co.uk/news/giant-ronan-joins-the-lions/}}</ref> After the initial short-term loan had ended, it was later reported on 22 July 2021 that he had returned to Swinton for the remainder of the 2021 season.

===York RLFC===
On 3 October 2022 he signed for [[York Knights|York RLFC]] on a two-year deal after having spent the previous season on loan with the club.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=3 October 2022|access-date=19 October 2022|title=Ireland prop Ronan Michael makes permanent move to Championship|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ronan-michael-ireland-york-city-knights/}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 29 August 2024 Michael signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] on a two-year deal from the start of the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24550058.bradford-bulls-sign-irish-ace-york-ocarroll-reunion/ |title=Bradford Bulls sign Irish ace from York in O'Carroll reunion |website=Telegraph & Argus |date=29 August 2024 |access-date=29 August 2024}}</ref>

On 11 April 2026 he was released from his contract with immediate effect through mutual agreement to allow him to pursue regular game time elsewhere<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 Apr 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Bradford Bulls grant Ireland ace immediate release from contract|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-bulls-grant-ireland-ace-immediate-release-from-contract}}</ref>

===Keighley Cougars (loan)===
Michael signed for [[Keighley Cougars]] on a one-month loan in February 2025.<ref>{{cite news |title=Bulls loan popular prop Michael to Keighley with game time limited |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/25870537.bradford-bulls-loan-ronan-michael-keighley-cougars/ |access-date=19 February 2026 |work=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |date=19 February 2026 |language=en}}</ref>

===Northern Pride===
On the 26th of April it was reported that he had signed for in the Northern Pride in the Hostplus Cup. On the 10th of May Ronan made his Pride debut in a 36-36 draw against Souths Logan Magpies.
